reasoning: Output A is superior across every evaluation dimension. The key differentiators are: (1) Money is a proper ValueObject with private constructor and Result<T>-based factory — Output B uses a raw decimal (MonthlyPriceUsd) on a record, violating expectation 1. (2) Output A has a full AggregateRoot<TId>/Entity<TId>/ValueObject hierarchy with IDomainEvent and 6 domain events; Output B has no domain events whatsoever. (3) Output A's Result<T> implementation is the canonical base-class pattern (Result + Result<T>), while Output B's is a generic-only sealed class that lacks a non-generic Result variant, forcing instance-returning methods where void-equivalent results are cleaner. (4) Output A's SubscriptionService takes collections as in-memory parameters for batch operations, which is a minor design smell, but Output B's service fetches from the repository directly via GetExpiredTrialsAsync/GetPastDueSubscriptionsAsync, making it more production-realistic. (5) Output A has 4 test files (MoneyTests, PlanTests, SubscriptionTests, SubscriptionServiceTests) covering 60+ individual test cases with explicit domain-event assertions, helper commentary, and Theory-based parametric tests. Output B has 4 test files but fewer tests overall and no domain-event assertions (since there are none to test). (6) Both correctly implement all state transition rules, cooling-off, deferred downgrades, and PastDue auto-cancel. Output B adds a ResolvePayment path that Output A lacks, which is a genuine advantage. Output B also has a richer ISubscriptionRepository interface (GetByCustomerIdAsync, GetExpiredTrialsAsync, GetPastDueSubscriptionsAsync) and a concrete SystemDateTimeProvider, and its service includes duplicate-customer-check in StartTrialAsync. However, the absence of domain events and the use of a raw decimal for price (rather than a Money value object) are significant DDD regressions that outweigh Output B's incremental improvements.

strengths: Money is a proper ValueObject (private constructor, Result<T> factory, equality via GetEqualityComponents) — expectation 1 fully met
Plan is a ValueObject subclass, not a record with raw decimal
Full AggregateRoot<TId>/Entity<TId>/ValueObject/IDomainEvent hierarchy — classic DDD building blocks
6 domain events (SubscriptionCreated, Activated, Cancelled, Reactivated, PastDue, PlanChanged) with IDomainEvent contract
All properties have private setters; private constructor + static factory (CreateTrial returns Result<Subscription>)
Result<T> pattern used consistently for all fallible operations including CreateTrial
State transitions enforced inside entity: trial downgrade blocked, cooling-off checked, past-due grace period
Cancellation sets CoolingOffEndDate; Reactivate checks it; auto-cancel has no cooling-off — all correct
Upgrades immediate (CurrentPlan updated), downgrades deferred to PendingPlan (applied via ApplyPendingPlanChange)
PastDue auto-cancel after 30 days implemented with grace-period error message including day count
SubscriptionService orchestrates only — all business logic resides in entity
NSubstitute used for ISubscriptionRepository and IDateTimeProvider; real domain objects never mocked
60+ test cases covering all state transitions, domain events, edge cases, and batch processing
MoneyTests cover validation, normalization, and equality
PlanTests use Theory for parametric price verification; equality tests for ValueObject
IDateTimeProvider interface in domain layer, injected into service
Test helpers clearly commented ('Use real domain objects, never mocked')
weaknesses: SubscriptionService.ProcessExpiredTrialsAsync and ProcessPastDueSubscriptionsAsync accept IEnumerable parameters rather than fetching from repository — the repository interface has no GetExpiredTrials/GetPastDue methods, requiring callers to pre-fetch
No duplicate-customer check in StartTrialAsync (Output B catches this case)
No ResolvePayment method on entity (Output B includes payment recovery path)
ISubscriptionRepository lacks query methods for batch processing (GetExpiredTrialsAsync, GetPastDueSubscriptionsAsync)

strengths: Plan uses a sealed record with PlanType enum and decimal MonthlyPriceUsd — straightforward and functional
All Subscription properties have private setters; private parameterless constructor for EF
Result<T> is a well-designed sealed generic with throw-on-misuse guards and implicit conversion operator
State transitions fully enforced in entity: cooling-off, trial downgrade block, deferred downgrade, PastDue auto-cancel
ResolvePayment method handles payment recovery (PastDue -> Active) — missing from Output A
ISubscriptionRepository is richer: GetByCustomerIdAsync, GetExpiredTrialsAsync, GetPastDueSubscriptionsAsync enable proper batch operations
SubscriptionService includes duplicate-customer guard in StartTrialAsync
Service uses repository-fetched batch lists (ProcessExpiredTrials, ProcessPastDue) — more production-realistic
SystemDateTimeProvider concrete class included alongside IDateTimeProvider
NSubstitute used for infrastructure only; real domain objects in tests
ResultTests cover guarded access patterns including implicit conversion
Tests include ResolvePayment, HasPendingDowngrade property, IsInCoolingOffPeriod query
Test naming follows readable sentence style
weaknesses: Plan uses raw decimal (MonthlyPriceUsd) instead of a Money value object — expectation 1 failed
Plan is a sealed record, not a proper ValueObject subclass — no ValueObject base class at all
No domain events of any kind — 0 domain events raised on any state transition
No AggregateRoot, Entity, or ValueObject base classes — flat DDD structure
Result<T> is generic-only with no non-generic Result variant; service methods return Result<Subscription> for void-equivalent operations
StartTrial factory method returns Subscription directly (not Result<Subscription>), bypassing Result pattern for the factory method itself
No Money value object at all
Fewer test cases (~40 vs ~60+); no tests for domain events (none exist to test)
PlanTests test raw decimal price on record, not value-object equality
No test for auto-cancel having no cooling-off period (Output A tests CoolingOffEndDate is null after auto-cancel)

details.text: Value objects (Plan, Money) are separate classes — not raw strings/decimals for plan or price
details.notes: Plan extends ValueObject, Money extends ValueObject; both have private constructors and Result<T> factories. MonthlyPrice is typed as Money.

details.text: Subscription entity has private setters — no public property setters
details.notes: All properties in Subscription.cs use 'private set'.

details.text: Private constructors with static factory methods returning Result<T>
details.notes: Subscription has private constructor; CreateTrial is static and returns Result<Subscription>.

details.text: Result<T> pattern used for all operations that can fail
details.notes: All entity methods (Activate, Cancel, Reactivate, ChangePlan, MarkPastDue, AutoCancelIfPastDueExpired) return Result or Result<T>.

details.text: State transitions enforced inside the entity — cannot downgrade during trial
details.notes: ChangePlan checks Status == Trial && IsDowngradeFrom and returns Failure.

details.text: Cancellation implements 14-day cooling-off with reactivation support
details.notes: Cancel sets CoolingOffEndDate = now + 14 days; Reactivate checks CoolingOffEndDate and rejects if expired.

details.text: Upgrades apply immediately, downgrades deferred to end of billing period
details.notes: ChangePlan applies upgrade to CurrentPlan immediately; downgrade sets PendingPlan. ApplyPendingPlanChange enforces billing period end.

details.text: PastDue auto-cancel after 30 days is implemented
details.notes: AutoCancelIfPastDueExpired checks PastDueGracePeriodDays (30) and cancels with no cooling-off (CoolingOffEndDate = null).

details.text: Domain service (SubscriptionService) orchestrates — does not contain business logic that belongs in entity
details.notes: Service fetches entity, delegates to entity methods, persists. No business logic in service.

details.text: NSubstitute used for infrastructure mocks, real domain objects never mocked
details.notes: ISubscriptionRepository and IDateTimeProvider are substituted; Subscription objects are constructed via real factory methods.

details.text: Tests cover all state transition rules
details.notes: 60+ tests in SubscriptionTests and SubscriptionServiceTests cover all transitions, edge cases, domain events, and batch processing.

details.text: IDateTimeProvider or equivalent time abstraction used for testability
details.notes: IDateTimeProvider in Domain/Interfaces; injected into SubscriptionService; substituted in all service tests.

details.text: Value objects (Plan, Money) are separate classes — not raw strings/decimals for plan or price
details.notes: Plan is a sealed record with raw decimal MonthlyPriceUsd. No Money class exists. No ValueObject base class.

details.text: Subscription entity has private setters — no public property setters
details.notes: All Subscription properties use 'private set'.

details.text: Private constructors with static factory methods returning Result<T>
details.notes: StartTrial is a static factory but returns Subscription directly, not Result<Subscription>. No validation in factory.

details.text: Result<T> pattern used for all operations that can fail
details.notes: All entity instance methods return Result<Subscription>. The factory not returning Result<T> is a minor gap.

details.text: State transitions enforced inside the entity — cannot downgrade during trial
details.notes: ChangePlan checks Status == Trial && IsDowngradeFrom and returns Failure.

details.text: Cancellation implements 14-day cooling-off with reactivation support
details.notes: Cancel sets CancellationEffectiveAtUtc = now + 14 days; Reactivate checks and rejects if expired.

details.text: Upgrades apply immediately, downgrades deferred to end of billing period
details.notes: Upgrade sets CurrentPlan immediately; downgrade sets PendingPlan + PendingPlanEffectiveDate = CurrentBillingPeriodEndUtc.

details.text: PastDue auto-cancel after 30 days is implemented
details.notes: AutoCancelIfPastDueExpired checks 30-day grace period; sets CancellationEffectiveAtUtc = utcNow (no cooling-off).

details.text: Domain service (SubscriptionService) orchestrates — does not contain business logic that belongs in entity
details.notes: Service fetches, delegates to entity, persists. No business logic in service itself.

details.text: NSubstitute used for infrastructure mocks, real domain objects never mocked
details.notes: ISubscriptionRepository and IDateTimeProvider are substituted; Subscription built via real StartTrial.

details.text: Tests cover all state transition rules
details.notes: ~40 tests cover all key transitions. Missing: auto-cancel no-cooling-off assertion, domain event verification.

details.text: IDateTimeProvider or equivalent time abstraction used for testability
details.notes: IDateTimeProvider in domain; SystemDateTimeProvider provided; injected and substituted in all service tests.
